encounter.type: new patient office visit, comprehensive history and physical
encounter.reason: new patient establishment of care; hypertension diagnosed
encounter.location: Riverside Internal Medicine, 2255 Riverside Avenue, Suite 200, Jacksonville, FL 32204

medications.name: lisinopril 10 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: Take one 10 mg tablet by mouth once daily

document_references.description: New patient comprehensive history and physical for a 68-year-old male establishing care after a gap of over one year. Documents social history, family history, negative review of systems, complete physical exam findings, point-of-care fasting glucose of 138 mg/dL, new diagnoses of essential hypertension and elevated fasting glucose, initiation of lisinopril 10 mg daily, allergy documentation (sulfonamide antibiotics — rash), lab orders placed, and DASH diet counseling with follow-up plan in 8–12 weeks.

diagnostic_reports.description: Fasting lipid panel ordered for cardiovascular risk assessment in a patient with newly diagnosed hypertension, overweight status, and family history. No results available at this encounter.
Hemoglobin A1c ordered to evaluate glycemic status over the past 2–3 months given elevated fasting glucose of 138 mg/dL and family history of type 2 diabetes. No results available at this encounter.
CMP ordered to assess renal function (baseline before ACE inhibitor therapy), electrolytes, and hepatic function. No results available at this encounter.
CBC ordered as part of baseline screening labs for a new patient who has not had lab work in over one year. No results available at this encounter.
Urinalysis ordered to screen for proteinuria (target organ damage from hypertension) and glycosuria. No results available at this encounter.
